rationale: EUR/USD is near a 1-year low at 1.1395, weighed by sticky US inflation sustaining USD strength, an ongoing oil surge that acts as a terms-of-trade drag on the EZ energy importer, and residual French political risk from Le Pen's poll-leading 2027 campaign. The July 23 ECB meeting — the key intra-horizon event — carries a dovish skew because any rate action is already priced, so a hold with cautious language would extend EUR weakness; however, the proximity to the 52-week low and potential crowded-short positioning caps conviction and creates two-way event risk.
key_drivers: USD supported by sticky US inflation / hawkish Fed backdrop
ECB July 23 meeting: rate priced in, asymmetric dovish risk
EUR near 1-year low / crowded-short risk limits downside conviction
Technical: below 50d and 200d SMA acting as resistance
